meaning: n.考虑将来,谋远,深虑,先见adj.预先计划好的,预谋的
examples: With a little forethought many accidents could be avoided.
Some forethought and preparation is necessary before you embark on the project.
Domestic noises may perhaps be controlled by forethought and courtesy, and industrial noises by good planning and technical improvement.
Strategery represented a flimsy veneer of forethought over a largely reactive and often ineffective game-plan.
The team was put together with little forethought about how the individuals would play together.
